name: VQIVYK-zipper-derived peptide inhibitors
mechanism: Steric-zipper-complementary peptide caps the VQIVYK dry interface, blocking templated growth at that segment
notes: Designed from the Sawaya/Eisenberg VQIVYK microcrystal structure (steric zipper class 1); only partially inhibits full-length-tau seeding per Seidler et al.

name: VQIINK-zipper-derived peptide inhibitors
mechanism: Steric-zipper-complementary peptide caps the VQIINK dry interface; VQIINK is reported the dominant nucleating segment, so these inhibitors block full-length tau seeding more effectively than VQIVYK-targeted ones
notes: Designed from cryo-EM/microED VQIINK microcrystal structures; exact designed sequences are given in the primary reference's supplementary tables and are not reproduced here to avoid mis-transcription — this table records the target segment and mechanism, which is what the M9 benchmark checks.

name: Methylene blue / methylthioninium (and stabilized reduced form LMTX/TRx0237)
target_segment: full-length tau (broad; not a single hexapeptide)
mechanism: Broad-spectrum aggregation inhibitor; oxidizes tau cysteines / disrupts beta-sheet stacking non-specifically rather than capping a single zipper interface
notes: Context/positive-control-adjacent compound cited in the brief; not segment-specific, so used as a negative control for segment-level specificity benchmarking (M9), not a positive hit for a single hexapeptide.

name: Epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG)
mechanism: Remodels tau fibrils into non-toxic amorphous aggregates via broad hydrophobic/hydrogen-bond interactions, not a single-motif mechanism
notes: Used as an additional broad-spectrum negative control for segment specificity (M9).
